Based on the search results, here's what I found about UNI, CONCH, BioMedCLIP, and medical image retrieval with volumetric embeddings in 2024:

## Foundation Models for Medical Image Retrieval

BioMedCLIP, CONCH, and UNI are among the pre-trained foundation models being evaluated as feature extractors for medical image retrieval. These models represent recent advances in medical imaging AI.

## Performance Comparison

For 2D medical image datasets, foundation models deliver superior performance compared to CNNs, with UNI providing the best overall performance across all datasets and image sizes. For 3D datasets, CNNs and foundation models deliver more competitive performance, with CONCH achieving the best overall performance.

UNI's superior performance is particularly interesting as it was only trained on histological images, yet it generalized well to other types of medical images, even outperforming BioMedCLIP, which was trained on various types of medical images.

## BioMedCLIP Details

BioMedCLIP pre-training was performed using the PMC-15M dataset containing around 15 million image–text pairs, employing a vision transformer (ViT-B/16-224) as the vision encoder and PubMedBERT with an extended token size of 256 as the text encoder.

## 3D Medical Image Retrieval Applications

3D image retrieval methods have been evaluated on datasets including Liver, Pancreas, Lung, and Colon using different embedding models including BiomedCLIP.

aiTitle: Train MLP for similarity scoring with DINO-v2 embeddings

content.text:
Here's a synthesis of what the literature says, ranked by relevance to your task (cross-modal T1↔T2 MRI retrieval, small training set, 3D volumes):

---

## Pretrained encoders

| Model | Type | Best for | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **RadDINO** (`microsoft/rad-dino`) | ViT-B, radiology DINO | MRI/X-ray | Already in your pipeline; strong domain fit |
| **MedImageInsight** (Microsoft, 2024) | ViT, text+image | CT 3D retrieval (claims SOTA), MRI | Open-source; trained across X-ray, CT, MRI, ultrasound |
| **BioMedCLIP** | ViT-B/16, 15M image-text pairs | 2D medical, generalises to 3D | 2nd best overall in benchmark; image+text aligned |
| **UNI** | ViT-L, histology | 2D best overall (79.45% ACC@1) | Surprisingly generalises; no 3D native support |
| **CONCH** | ViT, pathology+text | **Best for 3D** (72.68% ACC@1) | Beats all CNNs on volumetric retrieval |
| **M3Ret** (arXiv 2025) | ViT with unified 4D patchification | Zero-shot cross-modal MRI | Natively handles 2D/3D/video; SimDINO + MAE pretraining on 867K samples |

**Key finding from benchmark** ([arXiv 2409.09430](https://arxiv.org/html/2409.09430v1)): for 3D images, foundation models only marginally beat CNNs (~2%), unlike 2D where the gap is ~5%. The 3D problem is harder.

---

## Cross-modal specific techniques

**1. Projection heads + InfoNCE** — what you're already doing. Literature confirms this is the right approach for closing the T1/T2 modality gap with small labelled datasets.

**2. Masked contrastive reconstruction** ([arXiv 2312.15840](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2312.15840)) — mask patches in one modality, reconstruct from the other. Forces the encoder to learn shared structure across modalities. Would work well with your 350 pairs.

**3. M3Ret's unified patchification** — treats a 3D volume as a 4D tensor `(3, 16, 16, 4)` patches, same encoder for 2D slices and full 3D volumes. Zero-shot cross-modal retrieval without task-specific finetuning.

**4. ColBERT multi-vector re-ranking** — already in `strong_modal.py`. Confirmed by [arXiv 2507.17412](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.17412) as state-of-the-art for 3D medical retrieval.

---

## What's missing from your current pipeline

1. **MedImageInsight** — worth trying as a drop-in backbone replacement for RadDINO; it claims SOTA on CT 3D retrieval and explicitly supports MRI
2. **CONCH** — best benchmark result for 3D retrieval; not radiology-specific but may transfer
3. **Masked cross-modal reconstruction** as an auxiliary training loss on top of your InfoNCE — would better exploit the 350 training pairs
4. **M3Ret-style 3D-native encoding** — your current pipeline slices volumes and averages, losing inter-slice relationships; a 4D patch encoder keeps full volumetric context

---

The most actionable upgrade: swap **RadDINO → MedImageInsight** (same ViT-B size, open-source, explicitly trained on MRI + claims 3D CT SOTA) and add **masked cross-modal reconstruction** as an auxiliary loss alongside InfoNCE.

Sources:
- [Evaluating Pre-trained CNNs and Foundation Models for Medical Image Retrieval](https://arxiv.org/html/2409.09430v1)
- [M3Ret: Zero-shot Multimodal Medical Image Retrieval](https://arxiv.org/html/2509.01360v1)
- [Content-based 3D Image Retrieval and ColBERT Re-ranking](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2507.17412)
- [MedImageInsight: Open-Source Embedding Model for Medical Imaging](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.06542)
- [SuPreM: Supervised Pre-Trained 3D Models](https://github.com/MrGiovanni/SuPreM)
- [Content-Based Image Retrieval for Multi-Class Volumetric Radiology Images](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2405.09334)
